column1: # LatteX LaTeX-math test corpus — SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH.
# Both examples/corpus.md and CorpusParseTest.java derive from this file, so the
# documented tier and the empirically-verified parse outcome cannot drift.
# Columns are TAB-separated: TIER <tab> GROUP <tab> LATEX <tab> DESCRIPTION
# Backslashes are literal (this is a data file, not Java source — no escaping).
# TIER legend (see examples/corpus.md for the full rubric):
#   PARSES-NOW         parse() returns a tree today (verified by the test).
#   NEEDS-PARSER-NODE  parse() throws MathSyntaxException today; needs a new
#                      MathNode / parser feature (the DESCRIPTION names it).
#   NEEDS-S4-LAYOUT    parse() SUCCEEDS today, but faithful rendering needs new
#                      2D layout (delimiter sizing to content, eval bars).
#   NEEDS-FONT-STYLE   parse() throws today; the missing feature is fundamentally
#                      a font-variant glyph set or emitter color.
#   PARSER-BUG         parse() throws a NON-MathSyntaxException (NPE / SOE / CCE);
#                      a robustness bug — the parser should fail cleanly. (none found)

column4: sub and sup on the same base
nested fraction (braced args) — recursion of core frac layout
nested fraction, terse \frac1x form — brace-optional single-token \frac arguments
root of a fraction (braced) — recursion of core radical layout
diagonal fraction hack (empty-group scripts around a slash)
multi-line subscript via the \substack single-column stack
double-bar norm delimiters (\| maps to ‖, U+2016, like \Vert)
stretchy double-bar norm around tall content
scaled parens — S4 fencedBox stretches the delimiters to the fraction body
scaled braces — delimiters stretch symmetrically about the axis to the content
eval bar (braced) — null-left + stretched bar verified (LayoutS4Test.evalBarStretchesOverTheBody)
eval bar, terse \frac denominator — null-left + stretched bar verified (same layout path)
wide (stretchy) hat sized to the base
wide (stretchy) tilde sized to the base
phantom / hphantom / vphantom reserve space with no ink
mathstrut — a zero-width strut sizing the row
text runs — needs \text node (upright text mode)
piecewise cases — cases environment (left brace over two left-aligned columns)
colored subterm — \color group-scoped switch + emitter fill
plain matrix — environment with &/\\ grid node
bordered matrix with row/col labels — needs \bordermatrix node
array with vertical and horizontal rules — array node + column spec + \hline
two-equation align — alternating right/left column pair aligned on the relation
starred align (no equation numbers) — same rendering
derivation with an elided continuation LHS — empty first cell aligns on \=
several equations per line — two aligned relation columns with a wide inter-equation gap
display-style cells — integrals, fractions and big-op limits set full size
gather — each row centred, no & alignment
split — a single equation broken over lines, aligned on the relation (reuses the align machinery)
multline — one long equation: first line flush-left, last flush-right, middle centred
starred multline (no equation number) — same layout
limit and exp — \lim (under-limits in display) + roman \exp
cos and sin — roman named operators with beside scripts
bold beta vector — \boldsymbol remaps to bold math-alphanumeric (incl. Greek)
calligraphic/script alphabet — remaps to U+1D49C run (+ Letterlike exceptions)
blackboard-bold alphabet — remaps to U+1D538 run (C/H/N/P/Q/R/Z via Letterlike)
fraktur alphabet — remaps to U+1D504 run (C/H/I/R/Z via Letterlike)
math italic — remaps to U+1D434 run (h via Letterlike)
bold symbols — remaps to bold math-alphanumeric run
